title: BUG: Futu SDK enum strings vs integers in place_order calls
description:
## Background
The futu-api SDK's place_order method accepts string constants for enums (trd_side, order_type, time_in_force, trd_env) rather than integer enum values. Using integers causes runtime errors or unexpected behavior.

## Discovery
During Phase 3 paper-trading validation, an early test script used TrdSide.BUY (integer 0) which the SDK rejected. Switching to the string constant 'BUY' resolved the issue.

## Risk
- Any adapter code or test scripts using integer enums will fail at runtime.
- The v2 codebase may have ported code with integer enum usage.

## Fix
- Audit all place_order, modify_order, and cancel_order calls in src/sam_trader/adapters/futu/
- Ensure all enum parameters use string constants:
  - trd_side: 'BUY', 'SELL'
  - order_type: 'NORMAL', 'MARKET', etc.
  - time_in_force: 'DAY', 'GTC', etc.
  - trd_env: 'SIMULATE', 'REAL'
- Update test scripts and documentation examples.

## Files
- src/sam_trader/adapters/futu/execution.py
- tests/unit/adapters/futu/test_execution.py
- tests/integration/adapters/futu/test_execution_flow.py
- Any manual test scripts
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All place_order calls use string enums, not integers
- [ ] All modify_order and cancel_order calls use string enums where applicable
- [ ] Unit tests pass with string enum usage
- [ ] Integration test test_limit_order_lifecycle passes

title: BUG: Cross-network RSA encryption required when FUTU_OPEND_IP=0.0.0.0
description:
## Background
When Futu OpenD listens on 0.0.0.0 (the default for containerized deployment), Futu requires RSA encryption for the trading interface. Without it, the SDK can connect but trading operations fail with an encryption error.

## Discovery
During Phase 3 paper-trading validation, the first order submission failed until a 1024-bit RSA key was generated and mounted into both sam-futu-opend and sam-trader containers.

## Current state
- RSA key generated at docker/futu-opend/futu.pem
- Mounted to /.futu/futu.pem in both containers
- OpenD config: FUTU_OPEND_RSA_FILE_PATH=/.futu/futu.pem
- Python SDK: SysConfig.set_init_rsa_file('/.futu/futu.pem')

## Remaining work
- Document RSA key generation steps in FUTU_FIRST_LOGIN.md
- Consider automating key generation in start.py or deploy.sh if key is missing
- Ensure key is in .gitignore and never committed

## Files
- docker/futu-opend/futu.pem (runtime, gitignored)
- docker/futu-opend/start.py
- docs/user/FUTU_FIRST_LOGIN.md
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] FUTU_FIRST_LOGIN.md documents RSA key generation (ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 1024 -m PEM)
- [ ] docker-compose.yml mounts futu.pem into both sam-futu-opend and sam-trader
- [ ] .gitignore excludes *.pem files
- [ ] Optional: start.py warns if RSA key is missing when FUTU_OPEND_IP=0.0.0.0

title: BUG: sam-trader Dockerfile — futu-api log dir creation fails without writable /opt/sam_trader
description:
## Background
During Phase 3 paper-trading validation, the sam-trader container failed at runtime because futu-api (Futu Python SDK) attempts to create a log directory at /opt/sam_trader/.com.futunn.FutuOpenD, but the non-root sam user did not have write permission to /opt/sam_trader.

## Error observed
PermissionError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/opt/sam_trader/.com.futunn.FutuOpenD'

## Impact
- Any code path that imports or initializes futu-api crashes the container.
- Blocks all Futu trading operations.

## Fix (already applied in container)
Added RUN chown sam:sam /opt/sam_trader in docker/Dockerfile before USER sam.

## Remaining work
- Ensure this fix is committed and documented.
- Verify no other directories need similar treatment (e.g., ~/.cache, /tmp).

## Files
- docker/Dockerfile
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] docker/Dockerfile contains RUN chown sam:sam /opt/sam_trader before USER sam\n- [ ] Container starts and futu-api initializes without PermissionError\n- [ ] Fix committed to main branch

title: BUG: .env hostname staleness — v2→v3 migration left old container names
description:
## Background
During Phase 3 paper-trading validation, multiple connection failures occurred because .env contained stale container hostnames from the csam_trader_v2 migration.

## Stale values found
- csam-postgres → should be sam-postgres
- redis → should be sam-redis
- ib-gateway → should be sam-ib-gateway

## Impact
- sam-trader cannot connect to PostgreSQL, Redis, or IB Gateway if .env is not manually corrected.
- config.py falls back to defaults, but the defaults in .env.example were also stale.

## Fix
- Audit .env.example for all hostname references. Ensure every service name uses the sam- prefix.
- Add a validation script or pre-flight check that warns if hostnames do not match docker-compose service names.

## Files
- .env.example
- src/sam_trader/config.py (if defaults are hard-coded)
- scripts/ralph/ralph_preflight.sh (optional validation)
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] .env.example uses sam-postgres, sam-redis, sam-ib-gateway consistently\n- [ ] No stale csam-* or unqualified hostnames remain\n- [ ] Optional: pre-flight script detects hostname mismatches

title: P6: RealizedPnLTrackerActor — per-strategy realized P&L
description: An Actor that listens to OrderFilled events, computes realized P&L per strategy using FIFO matching, and persists to Redis. Provides a queryable API for circuit breakers (Phase 10) and dashboards. Resets at market open. Does NOT track unrealized P&L. This eliminates the ambiguous v2 max_daily_loss behavior where 9 triggers occurred because unrealized gains were partially offsetting realized losses.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Subscribes to OrderFilled events.
- [ ] Computes realized P&L per strategy_id using FIFO fill matching.
- [ ] Persists to Redis key sam:pnl:{strategy_id}:{date}.
- [ ] Exposes get_realized_pnl(strategy_id: str) -> Decimal method.
- [ ] Auto-resets at 00:00 UTC via clock.set_time_alert.
- [ ] Does NOT include unrealized P&L.
- [ ] Unit test: sequence of buys and sells → correct realized P&L.
- [ ] Unit test: reset at midnight → P&L returns to zero.
- [ ] Integration test: fill → Redis key updated within 1 second.

title: P6: RejectionMonitorActor — per-instrument rejection circuit breaker
description: An Actor that subscribes to OrderRejected events on the Nautilus message bus. Tracks consecutive rejections per (instrument_id, strategy_id, rejection_reason). After 3 consecutive identical rejections, emits a StrategyHaltRequest event. Strategies listen and stop submitting new orders for that instrument. A 15-minute cooldown allows periodic retry. This directly addresses the v2 NVDA Momentum issue where 189 rejections occurred over 9 hours with no self-halt.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Actor subscribes to OrderRejected events at on_start().
- [ ] Maintains in-memory counter per (instrument_id, strategy_id, reason).
- [ ] After max_consecutive_rejections (default 3) with same reason, emits StrategyHaltRequest.
- [ ] Strategy receives halt request → stops submitting new orders for that instrument.
- [ ] After cooldown_seconds (default 900), auto-clears counter and allows retry.
- [ ] Different rejection reasons do NOT aggregate.
- [ ] Unit test: 3 identical rejections → halt emitted. 2 rejections → no halt.
- [ ] Unit test: cooldown expires → counter reset → strategy resumes.

title: P5: Pre-flight IB account trading permissions check
description: On IB exec client connect, query account trading permissions via IB API. If the account lacks permissions required by active bundles (e.g., short selling for MomentumStrategy), emit a CRITICAL log and mark the strategy as disabled for that session. This prevents the v2 scenario where 189 short orders were rejected over 9 hours because the paper account lacked short-selling permissions.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] On IB exec client connect, query account permissions.
- [ ] If short-selling is disabled and an active bundle requires allowed_directions: [SHORT] or [LONG, SHORT], log CRITICAL and disable strategy.
- [ ] Disabled strategy does not submit orders but remains instantiated for observability.
- [ ] If permissions change on reconnect, re-evaluate and re-enable if permitted.
- [ ] Unit test: mock permission with shorts disabled → strategy disabled flag set.
- [ ] Unit test: mock permission with shorts enabled → strategy active.

title: IBKR post_only incompatibility — bracket orders rejected (v2 operational bug)
description:
## Background
During csam_trader_v2's first operational day (2026-05-20), 100% of bracket orders were rejected by Interactive Brokers. This resulted in 108 order rejections, 0 fills, and >USD 300 in missed paper-trading opportunity.

## Root Cause
NautilusTrader's `OrderFactory.bracket()` defaults `tp_post_only=True` for the take-profit limit leg, and `OrderFactory.limit()` defaults `post_only=True`. Interactive Brokers' TWS/Gateway API does not implement the `post_only` order attribute. When the TP leg was rejected, the OCA/OUO linkage caused the entire bracket (entry + SL + TP) to be rejected.

## v2 Fix
Explicitly set `tp_post_only=False` on all `bracket()` calls and `post_only=False` on all standalone `limit()` calls used for take-profit orders.

### Files patched in v2
- `src/csam_trader/strategies/orb.py` (5 lines)
- `src/csam_trader/strategies/momentum.py` (2 lines)
- `src/csam_trader/strategies/_template.py` (2 lines)

## What Must Happen in v3
- Port the fix into all v3 strategy files (orb, momentum, template).
- Consider a venue-aware order factory wrapper in `strategies/common.py` that sets IB-safe defaults automatically.
- Optionally enhance the IB adapter or config to validate/warn on `post_only=True` orders.

## Reference
- Fix doc: `~/Documents/ai_agent_docs/csam_trader_post_only_fix_2026-05-20.md`
- Nautilus docs: `OrderFactory.bracket()` `tp_post_only` parameter; `OrderFactory.limit()` `post_only` parameter
- IB API Reference: Order attributes (no `post_only` support)

title: P5: Remove non-standard custom IB exec client and factory
description:
## Problem

`src/sam_trader/adapters/ib/factories.py` defines `SamInteractiveBrokersLiveExecClientFactory` and `src/sam_trader/adapters/ib/exec_client.py` defines `PermissionCheckingIBExecutionClient`. These are custom implementations of Nautilus adapter components, violating the project rule that we use **only standard Nautilus-provided** data/exec clients and factories.

They are also **dead code** — never wired in `main.py` (which correctly uses the standard `InteractiveBrokersLiveExecClientFactory`). The custom `SamInteractiveBrokersLiveExecClientFactory` is never imported or registered in `build_trading_node()`.

## What to Fix

1. **Remove** `src/sam_trader/adapters/ib/factories.py` entirely
2. **Remove** `src/sam_trader/adapters/ib/exec_client.py` entirely
3. **Remove** `tests/unit/adapters/ib/test_exec_client.py`
4. **Update** `src/sam_trader/adapters/ib/__init__.py` — ensure no broken imports from removed modules; keep it clean (currently just a docstring — confirm it's valid after removal)

No wiring changes needed in `main.py` — it already imports and registers the standard Nautilus `InteractiveBrokersLiveExecClientFactory` and `InteractiveBrokersLiveDataClientFactory`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `src/sam_trader/adapters/ib/` contains only `__init__.py` and `constants.py`
- `rg "PermissionCheckingIBExecutionClient|SamInteractiveBrokersLiveExecClientFactory" src/` returns no results
- All remaining tests pass: `pytest tests/unit/adapters/ib/ tests/unit/test_main_ib_*.py -q`

title: P5: Integration test for standard IB execution path post-cleanup
description:
## Problem

After removing the custom IB exec client/factory (Bug #1) and dead permissions module (Bug #2), there is no integration test that validates the full Phase 5 execution path using **only standard Nautilus components**.

The existing `test_dual_venue.py` tests that both venues coexist, but needs enhancement to verify:
1. Only standard Nautilus factories are registered (not custom ones)
2. The strategy-level `post_only=False` fix via `strategies/common.py` is applied in a full TradingNode context
3. No import errors from removed modules

## What to Fix

Enhance `tests/integration/test_dual_venue.py` to add or augment:

1. **Standard factory assertion**: Verify the registered IB factories are exactly the standard Nautilus classes:
   - `InteractiveBrokersLiveDataClientFactory` (not a custom subclass)
   - `InteractiveBrokersLiveExecClientFactory` (not `SamInteractiveBrokersLiveExecClientFactory`)

2. **Strategy-level post_only guard in TradingNode context**: Create a test bundle with an IB-venue strategy. Load it through the real `build_trading_node()` path. Verify that:
   - The bundle loads with `exchange="SMART"`
   - `strategies/common.py` functions (`make_bracket`, `make_limit`) correctly set `post_only=False` when called with an IB-venue instrument (covered by existing `test_common.py`, but verify no regression)

3. **No dead imports**: Verify that importing `sam_trader.adapters.ib` does NOT import any removed modules:
   - `rg` check: no reference to `PermissionCheckingIBExecutionClient` or `SamInteractiveBrokersLiveExecClientFactory` in the import chain
   - `from sam_trader.adapters.ib import *` or explicit imports work without ImportError

4. **Post-cleanup smoke test**: The full dual-venue test (existing) continues to pass after Bug #1 and Bug #2 removals.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All integration tests pass: `pytest tests/integration/test_dual_venue.py -v`
- Standard Nautilus factory classes are verified
- No import of removed modules in any `__init__.py` or `main.py`
- Full Phase 5 EXIT criteria still validated: Futu + IB strategies coexist, data flows from both venues, no cross-contamination

title: P5: Remove dead ib_trading_mode field from SamTraderConfig
description:
## Problem

`SamTraderConfig` defines `ib_trading_mode: str` (config.py line 27) and reads `IB_TRADING_MODE` from env (config.py line 90). This field is **never used anywhere** in `main.py` or any IB wiring.

The standard Nautilus `InteractiveBrokersExecClientConfig` has **no `trading_mode` parameter** (confirmed via `inspect.signature`). The `TRADING_MODE` env var is only consumed by the IB Gateway Docker container (`docker-compose.yml` sam-ib-gateway line 176: `TRADING_MODE: ${IB_TRADING_MODE:-paper}`), not by any Nautilus Python code.

The dead field is misleading — it implies the Nautilus client uses it, but it doesn't.

## What to Fix

1. Remove `ib_trading_mode` from `SamTraderConfig` dataclass fields (config.py line 27)
2. Remove `ib_trading_mode` from `from_env()` constructor call (config.py line 90)
3. Update `tests/unit/test_config.py` — remove/update tests that reference `ib_trading_mode` (lines 66, 138, 175, 221)
4. In `.env.example`, keep `IB_TRADING_MODE=paper` but **add a comment** clarifying it's consumed by the IB Gateway Docker container (via docker-compose.yml), not by sam-trader Python code. This avoids future confusion.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `SamTraderConfig` has no `ib_trading_mode` field
- `rg "ib_trading_mode" src/` returns no results
- `.env.example` still has `IB_TRADING_MODE=paper` with an explanatory comment
- `test_config.py` updated (no references to `ib_trading_mode`)
- All tests pass

title: P5: Fix silent fallback on invalid IB_MARKET_DATA_TYPE
description:
## Problem

In `src/sam_trader/main.py` (lines 142-146):
```python
market_data_type = getattr(
    IBMarketDataTypeEnum,
    cfg.ib_market_data_type,
    IBMarketDataTypeEnum.REALTIME,
)
```

`getattr()` silently falls back to REALTIME for any invalid value. A typo like `IB_MARKET_DATA_TYPE=DELAYD` would silently become REALTIME, causing wrong market data subscriptions with **no operator visibility**.

## What to Fix

Replace the silent `getattr` fallback with an explicit check that logs a WARNING before falling back:

```python
if hasattr(IBMarketDataTypeEnum, cfg.ib_market_data_type):
    market_data_type = getattr(IBMarketDataTypeEnum, cfg.ib_market_data_type)
else:
    logger.warning(
        "IB_MARKET_DATA_TYPE=%r is not a valid MarketDataTypeEnum value. "
        "Valid: %s. Falling back to REALTIME.",
        cfg.ib_market_data_type,
        [e.name for e in IBMarketDataTypeEnum],
    )
    market_data_type = IBMarketDataTypeEnum.REALTIME
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Unit test: set `IB_MARKET_DATA_TYPE=INVALID`, assert WARNING is logged and REALTIME is used
- Unit test: set `IB_MARKET_DATA_TYPE=DELAYED`, assert no WARNING, DELAYED is used
- All existing IB config tests continue to pass

title: P5: Fix .env.example WAIT_FOR broker defaults mismatch
description:
## Problem

`docker/docker-compose.yml` defaults both `WAIT_FOR_IB_GATEWAY` and `WAIT_FOR_FUTU_OPEND` to `1` (wait for broker gateway at startup), but `.env.example` sets both to `0` (skip wait).

An operator copying `.env.example` → `.env` will get `=0`, causing the entrypoint to skip the TCP-ping wait. The Nautilus client will then attempt connection before the gateway container is ready, causing startup failures.

**Relevant files:**
- `.env.example` lines 34-35: `WAIT_FOR_IB_GATEWAY=0`, `WAIT_FOR_FUTU_OPEND=0`
- `docker/docker-compose.yml` lines 34, 44: `WAIT_FOR_IB_GATEWAY=${WAIT_FOR_IB_GATEWAY:-1}`, `WAIT_FOR_FUTU_OPEND=${WAIT_FOR_FUTU_OPEND:-1}`
- `docker/entrypoint.sh` lines 48, 70: `${WAIT_FOR_IB_GATEWAY:-0}`, `${WAIT_FOR_FUTU_OPEND:-0}`

## What to Fix

1. In `.env.example`, change `WAIT_FOR_IB_GATEWAY=0` → `WAIT_FOR_IB_GATEWAY=1`
2. In `.env.example`, change `WAIT_FOR_FUTU_OPEND=0` → `WAIT_FOR_FUTU_OPEND=1`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `.env.example` lines 34-35 read `=1` not `=0`
- `rg "WAIT_FOR.*=0" .env.example` returns no results

title: P5: Remove dead permission checking infrastructure
description:
## Problem

`src/sam_trader/adapters/ib/permissions.py` provides `PermissionGuardMixin`, `query_ib_permissions()`, `disable_bundles_missing_permissions()`, `DISABLED_BUNDLE_IDS`, and `is_bundle_disabled()`. All of these depend on having a hook into the exec client's `_account_summary` and `submit_order` path.

Since Bug #1 removes the custom exec client/factory (we must use the standard Nautilus `InteractiveBrokersExecutionClient` — no subclassing allowed), this entire module has **no hook point** and is dead code.

Additionally:
- `set_bundle_permission_requirements()` is **never called** from `main.py`
- `query_ib_permissions()` requires `_account_summary` data only available after exec client connect, which can't be accessed without a custom subclass
- `PermissionGuardMixin` was intended for Phase 7 strategies but relies on the dead `DISABLED_BUNDLE_IDS` set

The permission-check functionality will be properly re-implemented in Phase 6 as a standard Nautilus **Actor** that subscribes to account state events.

## What to Fix

1. **Remove** `src/sam_trader/adapters/ib/permissions.py` entirely
2. **Remove** `tests/unit/adapters/ib/test_permissions.py`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `src/sam_trader/adapters/ib/` contains only `__init__.py` and `constants.py`
- `rg "permissions" src/sam_trader/adapters/ib/` returns no results
- All remaining tests pass

description:

title: BUG: docker-compose.yml sam-trader service missing critical env var mappings
description:
## Gap

The `sam-trader` service in `docker/docker-compose.yml` does not pass several environment variables that are defined in `.env.example` and read by `src/sam_trader/config.py`. This means settings in `.env` are silently ignored at container runtime.

Missing env vars in `sam-trader` compose service:
- `IB_ENABLED` — controls whether IBKR clients are registered
- `IB_TRADING_MODE` — controls paper vs live mode
- `FUTU_ENABLED` — controls whether Futu clients are registered
- `FUTU_TRD_ENV` — controls SIMULATE vs REAL trading
- `FUTU_TRD_MARKET` — controls US vs HK market
- `FUTU_UNLOCK_PWD_MD5` — required for unlocking Futu trade
- `BUNDLES_PATH` — path to strategy bundle registry

## Risk

- Users cannot enable/disable brokers via `.env`.
- Futu trading environment/market cannot be configured via `.env`.
- Strategy bundle path cannot be overridden via `.env`.
- `config.py` falls back to hard-coded defaults, causing surprise behavior.

## Fix

Add the missing environment variable mappings to the `sam-trader` service in `docker/docker-compose.yml`, following the existing pattern:



## Labels
phase-0, bug, docker, configuration

title: BUG: Futu OpenD service image and env vars deviate from ticket AC
description:
## Gap

Ticket sam_trader-9z3.1.7 acceptance criteria specifies:
- Image: `futuopen/futu-opend:latest`
- Env vars: `FUTU_LOGIN_ACCOUNT`, `FUTU_LOGIN_PWD_MD5`, `FUTU_TRADE_PASSWORD`, `FUTU_RSA_PRIVATE_KEY`
- Volume: `futu_opend_data:/data`

Actual `docker-compose.yml` uses:
- Image: `ghcr.io/manhinhang/futu-opend-docker:ubuntu-stable`
- Env vars: `FUTU_ACCOUNT_ID`, `FUTU_ACCOUNT_PWD_MD5` (missing `FUTU_TRADE_PASSWORD`, `FUTU_RSA_PRIVATE_KEY`)
- Volume: `futu_opend_data:/home/futu/.com.futunn.FutuOpenD`

Additionally, the chosen `manhinhang` image requires an RSA private key file mounted at `/.futu/futu.pem`, which is not present in the compose stack. Without it, the container will fail to authenticate.

## Risk

- Ticket acceptance criteria are not met.
- Missing RSA key mount will block Futu OpenD authentication at runtime.
- Env var naming inconsistency between ticket, `.env.example`, and compose.

## Fix Options

**Option A** (match ticket): Switch to `futuopen/futu-opend:latest` and align env vars/volume per ticket AC.

**Option B** (keep better image): Update ticket AC to match the chosen `manhinhang` image, add RSA key volume mount (e.g. `./config/futu.pem:/.futu/futu.pem:ro`), and ensure `.env.example` documents `FUTU_ACCOUNT_PWD_MD5` correctly.

## Labels
phase-0, bug, docker, futu

title: BUG: Dockerfile base image not pinned to Nautilus v1.227.0
description:
## Gap

Ticket sam_trader-9z3.1.3 acceptance criteria specifies:
- `FROM ghcr.io/nautechsystems/nautilus_trader:1.227.0`

Actual `docker/Dockerfile` uses:
- `FROM ghcr.io/nautechsystems/nautilus_trader:latest`

## Risk

- `latest` tag may drift to a newer/incompatible Nautilus version.
- `pyproject.toml` pins `nautilus_trader==1.227.0` but the container base image is unbounded.
- Breaks reproducible builds and CI cache stability.

## Fix

Change Dockerfile line 3 to:
`FROM ghcr.io/nautechsystems/nautilus_trader:1.227.0`

## Labels
phase-0, bug, docker

notes:
=== GAP REMEDIATION SCOPE (added 2026-05-22) ===
Derived from v2 post-mortem 21-May: 189 rejections with no self-halt; max_daily_loss triggered 9x with ambiguous unrealized P&L behavior.

ADDED SCOPE — Circuit Breaker Trigger Sources:
Expand from 3 to 5 triggers:
1. DAILY_PNL (existing)
2. MARGIN_LIMIT (existing)
3. CONNECTIVITY_LOSS (existing)
4. REJECTION_STREAK (new) — triggered by StrategyHaltRequest from RejectionMonitorActor (sam_trader-9z3.7.7)
5. REALIZED_LOSS_LIMIT (new) — uses RealizedPnLTrackerActor.get_realized_pnl() (sam_trader-9z3.7.8)

The REALIZED_LOSS_LIMIT trigger uses purely realized P&L, eliminating ambiguous v2 behavior where unrealized gains offset daily-loss calculations. REJECTION_STREAK auto-resets when RejectionMonitorActor cooldown expires.

ADDED ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] REJECTION_STREAK: simulate 3 identical rejections → RejectionMonitorActor emits halt → SafetyController triggers circuit breaker → new orders blocked.
- [ ] REALIZED_LOSS_LIMIT: simulate realized loss exceeding threshold → circuit breaker trips. Unrealized P&L does NOT affect calculation.
- [ ] Daily reset at 00:00 UTC clears all circuit breaker states.
- [ ] Integration test: full flow from fill → PnLTracker → SafetyController → circuit breaker.

- [ ] allowed_directions=["LONG", "SHORT"] → both directions trade.
- [ ] allowed_directions=["LONG"] → short signals skipped, long signals execute.
- [ ] allowed_directions=["SHORT"] → long signals skipped, short signals execute.
- [ ] Entry order type configurable (same as OrbStrategy).
- [ ] Unit test: short signal with allowed_directions=["LONG"] → no order submitted.
- [ ] Unit test: long signal with allowed_directions=["LONG"] → order submitted.

title: EPIC: SAM Trader V3
description:
## Goal
Ground-up rebuild of SAM Trader V3 trading platform on NautilusTrader v1.227.0. FUTUBULL as primary broker, IBKR as secondary. Multi-venue, multi-strategy, decoupled operations.

## Architecture
sam-postgres + sam-redis + sam-futu-opend + sam-ib-gateway (opt) + sam-trader (TradingNode) + sam-services (operations)

## Phases (11)
0. Foundation — Skeleton & Docker Stack
1. Configuration & Bootstrap
2. Futu Market Data Adapter
3. Futu Execution Adapter
4. Futu Instrument Provider & TradingNode Integration
5. IBKR Adapter Re-integration
6. Actors & State Management
7. Strategy Library & Bundle System
8. sam-services Container
9. Pre-Market Pipeline
10. Safety & Dashboard
11. Deploy Script & E2E Validation

## Reference
- Plan: docs/reference/SAM_TRADER_V3_PLAN.md
- Tickets: docs/agent/TICKET_PLAN_V3.md
